turns.user_message: I noticed lately how quickly I apologize for taking up space, which is funny because that never used to bother me at all. Back then, I just thought I was being polite or considerate. Now it feels like a heavy little weight I carry around in my chest. I catch myself muttering sorry when someone bumps into me or when I ask a simple question. It is just a weird shift that I cannot quite shake off today.

I keep noticing how that same habit of apologizing has completely taken over my daily routine, and honestly it used to not bother me in the slightest. I used to laugh it off as just part of my personality. Lately though, it feels like I am constantly trying to shrink myself out of existence. Every time I speak up or make a choice, I immediately backtrack and offer excuses. I am starting to feel deeply ashamed of how much room I take up in the world.

I finally realized how deeply ingrained this constant need to apologize has become, which is wild because it genuinely used to not bother me at all. I used to think it was harmless, but now it just feels like proof of how fundamentally flawed I am. I spend hours replaying every minor interaction and cringing at how much I shrink myself to make others comfortable. It is exhausting to carry around this quiet shame for simply existing. I just feel like I am permanently broken beyond any easy fix.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This used to not bother the speaker.
